title: gordonia samuelssonii
intro: Gordonia samuelssonii, also known as Qwalwal, is a traditional medicinal plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Cunoniaceae. It has been used by the Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of respiratory infections. Gordonia samuelssonii is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, although its availability and usage may be limited due to its native habitat and potential conservation status.
